Document.title: Document
Document.description: Document or artifact containing the described falsobordone.

id.title: Reference ID
id.description: Name by which this document will be referenced within this database.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name by which the source is commonly referred to.

archive.title: Archive
archive.description: Current archive the manuscript is located at.

witness.title: Witness
witness.description: Type of source in which the music survives.

anyOf.title: Print
anyOf.description: An early print (select only if other more specific print categories are not suitable).

anyOf.title: Performance
anyOf.description: A recording or a transcription of an oral transmission.

anyOf.title: Other
anyOf.description: An unusual document (e.g. a set of knifes :) ).

prevalentPartLayout.$comment: TO DO: This is bad, most likely remove it.
prevalentPartLayout.title: Part Layout (prevalent)
prevalentPartLayout.description: How voices are (most of the time) organized in this source.

links.title: Links
links.description: Links containings data related to the source.

PartLayout.title: Part Layout
PartLayout.description: How the voices are organized in the source.
PartLayout.$comment: TO DO: maybe borrow from MEI's @notationtype..? or something similar.

anyOf.title: Choirbook
anyOf.description: Source has parts (voices) written separately onto distinctive page regions.

anyOf.title: Partbook
anyOf.description: Each voice is contained in a separate book (or e.g. a set of cuttlery items).

anyOf.title: Modern score
anyOf.description: A modern score (has barlines and voices are vertically aligned).

anyOf.title: Partitura
anyOf.description: Voices are aligned one under another (often without strict vertical alignment).

anyOf.title: Other
anyOf.description: Other arrangement of voices (please describe it). Note: only use this option if really necessary.

Tonus.$comment: TO DO: refine it with literature, sources.
Tonus.title: Tonus

tonusID.title: Tonus ID
tonusID.description: Number of tonus (as a Latin number or label).
tonusID.$comment: TO DO: move it to another database

variantID.title: Tonus termination variant
variantID.description: Reference to termination variant of the psalmtone.

Falsobordone.title: Falsobordone
Falsobordone.description: A record containing information about falsobordone.

titleInDocument.title: Title (as in document)
titleInDocument.description: How the music is entitled in the source.

layout.title: Part layout
layout.description: How are the composition's parts (voices) layed out in the source.

Analysis.title: Analysis
Analysis.description: Data derived from the transcribed source (but not necessarily contained in (or directly derivable from) the MEI file, from which they cannot be trivially derived. Per hemistich.

cantusFirmus.title: Cantus Firmus
cantusFirmus.description: How is cantus firmus treated in the composition

part.title: Part label (standardized)
part.description: Which part is it (note: the original voice labeling should be provided within the MEI file.

TonalContent.title: Part (Voice) Tonal Content
TonalContent.description: Information about the tonal content of a part (voice).

intonatio.title: Intonatio
intonatio.description: Very first written pitch of the part.

tenor.title: Recit pitch (tenor)
tenor.description: The written pitch corresponding to cantus firmus's tenor (reciting) pitch.

HemistichSegment.title: Hemistich Segment
HemistichSegment.description: Analysis of a segment within the hemistich.
HemistichSegment.$comment: TO DO: rename other 'tonal' things to 'segment'...?

pitch.description: The written pitch (corresponding to cantus firmus note)

TonalOutline.title: Tonal Outline
TonalOutline.description: Information about the tonal outline of Falsobordone. Note: if cf is in bass, fill only "tenor".

bass.title: Bass (structural)
bass.description: Information about the lowest structural notes.

Part.title: Part (or voice)
Part.description: A description of a single part of a composition.

location.title: Part's location within a document
location.description: Where exactly the parts are located within a document.

folio.title: Folio
folio.description: On which folio(s) this part is written.

staff.title: Staff number annotation
staff.description: On which folio's staff the voice is written (counting from 1) OR the staff closest to preceeding part position. Note: empty staves also count.

staffAnnotation.title: Staff position comment
staffAnnotation.description: If the part is located on a staff in such a way that providing an exact staff number is problematic, provide a comment.
examples: An additional handwritten staff between 3rd and 4th staff on the print's page. [Note: 'staff' is 3]
An additional handwritten staff on the page margin. [Note: 'staff' is 3]

partNameInDocument.title: Part name (in document)
partNameInDocument.description: Part's name within the document. Can contain TEI. NOTE: Leave empty if part is not named.

tonusInDocument.title: Tonus (as in document)
tonusInDocument.description: Tonus label within the document. Note: may contain TEI.

ambitus.title: Ambitus
ambitus.description: Lowest and highest note of the part.

links.title: Links
links.description: Links containing data related to this part.

items.$comment: TO DO: make this schema 'extend' Link schema such that it has compulsory type 'image', 'IFFF' or 'audio' (?) for linkType property.

PartID.title: Part name (standardised)
PartID.description: A conventional name of this part. (Note: if part name is not given in the source, use the closest but most common label (a.g. 'bass', not 'baricanor').
PartID.$comment: TO DO: use some more formal convention.

PitchID.title: Pitch ID
PitchID.description: A letter name of a written pitch.

PitchOctaveID.title: Pitch with octave ID
PitchOctaveID.description: A letter name of a written pitch with octave number. (Note: C4 = middle C).

ComposedOut.title: Composed-out
ComposedOut.description: To what degree the melody has been composed.

annotation.title: Annotations
annotation.description: Any remarks about the content within the link.
